title: Who knew about Gendos affairs
wikitext:
[]: Who knew about Gendo's affairs?             Quote Post by pioneer » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:12 pm Did anyone in NGE know about Gendo's affairs with the Akagi women? When did Gendo's affair with Naoko begin? Before or after Yui died? Did Yui know about it? If she did, I think that's why she bit Gendo in half in EOE instead of letting him get tang'd with everyone else. Does anyone know any concrete details regarding this or have any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:45 pm I think Gendo's relationship with Naoko Akagi only happened after Yui's death, around 2010, and Ritsuko was definitely aware of it to some extent. As for Ritsuko, it was only revealed very late into the series, and its knowledge to the other NERV staff would probably be sketchy at best. When you asked if Yui would have ever been aware of these affairs, I thought the soul was dormant in the Eva, despite the two (Gendo and Ritsuko) often appearing in tandem around Unit-01.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:53 pm Replicator wrote: I think Gendo's relationship with Naoko Akagi only happened after Yui's death, around 2010, and Ritsuko was definitely aware of it to some extent. As for Ritsuko, it was only revealed very late into the series, and its knowledge to the other NERV staff would probably be sketchy at best. When you asked if Yui would have ever been aware of these affairs, I thought the soul was dormant in the Eva, despite the two (Gendo and Ritsuko) often appearing in tandem around Unit-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:01 pm pioneer wrote: Yui's death was not in 2010, from the wiki: She died in 2004 as a result of her Contact Experiment with Eva-01, but her spirit lives on within the Eva as eternal proof of humanity's existence. Also, I'm not sure what makes you think Yui's soul is 'dormant' in unit 01. The only time a soul is dormant is when Kaworu explicitly states so in regards to unit 02.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:14 pm I'm trying to remember if Shinji found out. I think he was told when Ritsuko destroyed the Rei clones, but I'm not 100% sure. If he knew, then maybe Yui could have found out that way, but I'm not sure how that works either.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:59 pm Replicator wrote: Well, yes. I'm saying Yui's death was in 2004, but we aren't shown Gendo and Naoko's affair until 2010, which was when the MAGI were being completed, and Gehirn was reorganized into NERV. Unless Evangelions have some sort of ability to perceive their surroundings while in containment, how would Yui find out about what Gendo is up to when she is inside of the Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Thu Dec 13, 2012 11:03 pm pioneer wrote: Shoot! I forgot that those scenes are in 2010, sorry! 01 moves in episode 1 to protect Shinji, showing that 01 is able to perceive her surroundings while in containment. but i'm not sure how she would gather that gendo is cheating on her.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Dec 13, 2012 11:17 pm Well Ritsuko knew about her mother of course....because if she's gonna share something with her mother, it might as well be Gendo's boloney pony. In all seriousness though: I'll throw in Kaji for a potential......he's a spy, so he might have found out that kind of information. Maybe he could have let slip some of that to Misato, or more probably, SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 14, 2012 2:23 am Naoko's comments in episode 21 about the contact experiment suggested that she moved in pretty swiftly to console the grieving widower.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 14, 2012 2:58 am Replicator wrote: Unless Evangelions have some sort of ability to perceive their surroundings while in containment, how would Yui find out about what Gendo is up to when she is inside of the Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Dec 14, 2012 3:22 am We know for sure Ritsuko knew about Naoko and Gendo's affair (see episode 21) And Gendo only started the affair with the Akagis after Yui "died", in fact his purpose for seducing the Akagis wasn't so much escaping loneliness/grief (although i would imagine that was a good part of it, specially with Naoko) But rather utilize them in the Instrumentality plan, in which his version involved getting Yui back. He doesn't care for the Akagis outside of that. Which makes me wonder if he feels any guilt or related feelings about the way he's using them. It is possible that Yui is aware of Gendo's affairs, although we can't know for sure. Regardless, even if she knew i don't think she would care much for it considering she has other matters to attend to (Instrumentality, her agenda, stopping SEELE, etc.) And i seriously doubt she would jeopardize any of those matters for petty revenge (in reference to one of the comments in this thread of Yui "flicking [Ritsuko] with her finger). The idea of Kaji finding about it is good though, certainly likely. Also, i very seriously doubt that Yui bitted Gendo in half in EoE because of that, in fact, even if she knew about it, i highly doubt that even entered into the equation. Why she bitted him, though, i have no idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 14, 2012 4:25 am Dream wrote: Why she bitted him, though, i have no idea.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Sat Dec 15, 2012 10:43 pm Thanks for the thoughtful responses, and thanks for linking to that thread, Reichu. Does anyone think Fuyu had any idea about Gendo's affairs? Would it be of any consequence?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Dec 16, 2012 3:13 am You're welcome, as for the question: Fuyutsuki might or might not have known, i can see it going either way really. As for it being of any consequence, i very highly doubt that Fuyutsuki would have had the possibility or inclination/intention to do anything about it, i wouldn't be surprised if he didn't care at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 17, 2012 11:44 am Mr. Tines wrote: Naoko's comments in episode 21 about the contact experiment suggested that she moved in pretty swiftly to console the grieving widower.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Dec 17, 2012 2:33 pm I think it's pretty clear he cared nothing for Naoko, but I'd like to think it wasn't all just an act with Ritsuko. As to who knew about it... the section 2 guys in his and Akagi's escorts (assuming they have them), Fuyu, Keel obviously... maybe Kaji? Probably not anyone else. I doubt any of the bunnies know.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 17, 2012 2:35 pm Oh, yeah you can tell that Naoko had the hots for Gendo. In fact a common idea when people first watch the series, and are trying to piece things together is that she caused Yui's accident. Since it seems like she had motive. She certinally didn't shed any tears about Yui. Too bad that despite all their intelligence Naoko and Ritsuko could never quite comprehend that Gendo will only ever love one woman.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 17, 2012 4:10 pm NemZ wrote: I'd like to think it wasn't all just an act with Ritsuko.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Mon Dec 17, 2012 7:08 pm oh wow Kendrix, excellent post! I don't think Misato and Shinji were able to figure out who Ritsuko was referring to because they were facing the horror that Rei is actually a bunch of clones, and then immediately facing the next horror that all her clones were being destroyed. As we can see in 24 and 25', Shinji is wrapped up in his own troubles with Kaworu, Rei, and his mother, it seems like at that point, he is not considering what's going on with Gendo, let alone Ritsuko. On the other hand, Misato's relationship with Ritsuko seems to have changed a lot from that moment. In 25', Misato's absence is explained to other characters, but I do notice that Ritsuko slips away, while the bridge bunnies and Fuyu are distracted. At this point, it seems like no one is thinking about what's going on with Ritsuko =[

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 17, 2012 8:17 pm Gendo might not be the dominant type, but he can project that image. Remember he was in charge at Nerv. He can act authoritivly. But for how he is with Yui... Well look at the AU sequence in episode 26. "She who must be obeyed." But Gendo buried that part when he lost Yui, just like he tried to suppresse all his feelings for Shinji. Naoko I don't know. She clearly had the hots for Gendo since before Yui dissappered. As a scientist she was brilliant. As a mother, subpar. As a woman... I don't really know what to say. Now Ritsuko... Ugh. She's an idiot. She has to have seen what happened to her mother. She has to have realized that is was not a healthy relationship. And she should have realized that somehow it tied into her mother's death. And screwing the guy your mother was is just plain creepy. But she seems to have deluded herself that Gendo was what she thought he was. The tall dark and handsome guy with the broken heart she can heal. Well sorry, not that last part.
